description: Phoenix Security Blue Shield - Firewall — Codex CLI hook configuration
instructions: Place in ~/.codex/hooks.json. IMPORTANT: the handler 'path' must be an ABSOLUTE path — Codex resolves it relative to ~/.codex, so a relative path silently fails to run (no enforcement). Either run `npx @phoenix-security/cli install-hooks codex` (writes an absolute path automatically) or replace ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO below with the absolute path to your checkout. Set PHOENIX_API_KEY; fail mode is CLOSED by default (see PHOENIX_FAIL_OPEN / PHOENIX_REQUIRE_KEY / PHOENIX_STRICT).

handler.description: Evaluates packages against the Phoenix firewall before install. Reuses the same script (and shared library) as Claude Code. The broad 'match' fires the hook for any package-manager command; the script's parser makes the precise allow/deny decision, so short forms like `npm i` / `pip3 install` are not bypasses.

on_deny.message: Package blocked by Phoenix Security Blue Shield - Firewall policy.
